vitals.location: Poland (national circumnavigation, honorary start Warsaw Royal Wilanów)
vitals.start_format: Individual departures at 1-4 minute intervals; drafting prohibited
vitals.entry_fee: 2026 solo unsupported (3,600km): EUR160-256; solo supported: EUR410-656; 2-person team: EUR533-853; 4-person team: EUR693-1,109
vitals.cutoff_time: Solo: 3,600km 12 days, 1,800km 6 days, 900km 3 days. Teams: 3,600km 8 days, 1,800km 4 days, 900km 2 days. Challenge 300: 24 hours
route_options: RAP (full): 3,600 km / 33,100m — solo unsupported, solo supported, 2-/4-person team
1/2 RAP: 1,800 km, finishing near Grudza/Szklarska Poręba
Challenge 300: 150 km out-and-back to Czemierniki (solo unsupported, solo semi-supported, 2-person team)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): this is an extreme self-supported/supported ultracycling national circumnavigation (3,600km/33,100m, 100-athlete field in 2026), not a conventional gran_fondo — field_depth=2, community_culture=3 and other fondo-shaped dimensions were scored against ultra-format assumptions. Recommend a human re-check the discipline/scoring fit (see catalog_flags taxonomy note). Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: A full national circumnavigation ultracycling time trial, honorary-started at Warsaw's Royal Wilanów with a timed start outside the city at Obórki, looping the entire Polish border: the eastern frontier via Włodawa and the Ukrainian border, the Bieszczady and Beskids, Czorsztyn and the Tatras, the Karkonosze/Sudetes, the German border, the Baltic coast, Masuria, and the Lithuania/Belarus border area before returning to Warsaw. Riders start individually at 1-4 minute intervals with drafting prohibited — this is an individual time trial, not a mass-start race.
course_description.signature_challenge: The full 3,600 km/33,100m loop itself — no single climb or sector defines the race; the challenge is sustaining pace and sleep management across 12 days (solo) through eight distinct Polish regions.
suffering_zones: The eastern border stretch along the Ukrainian frontier — remote, exposed riding early in the loop
The Bieszczady, Beskids and Tatras (Czorsztyn) — the route's main climbing zones
The Baltic coast and Masuria sections — long, exposed flat riding late in the loop when fatigue has accumulated

logistics.lodging_strategy: Solo unsupported riders arrange their own rest stops at stationary support points or casual accommodations; supported and team categories use a crew and support vehicle.

history.origin_story: First edition run in 2021 as a full Polish national-circumnavigation ultracycling race. Shorter 1,800km (1/2 RAP) and 900km (1/4 RAP) distances, plus the 150km Challenge 300, were introduced later to provide progression paths below the full distance.
notable_moments: 2026: Sixth edition, 100 athletes from 12 countries, including 58 in the 3,600km solo-unsupported WUCA championship category

biased_opinion.verdict: A Genuine National Circumnavigation Ultra, Not a Gran Fondo
biased_opinion.summary: Race Around Poland is a real, well-organized extreme ultracycling event — a full 3,600km/33,100m loop of the entire country, first run in 2021 and now hosting a genuine WUCA solo-unsupported world championship category. It offers real progression (150km Challenge 300, 900km 1/4 RAP, 1,800km 1/2 RAP, full 3,600km) and both supported and unsupported options. But with only 100 athletes across every distance in 2026, it should never be presented as a large-field mass-participation fondo — it's a small, serious ultracycling field tackling one of Europe's most complete national circumnavigations, and its own website is inconsistent about exactly how many category types it offers (the rules say seven, the homepage says eight).
strengths: An unusually complete national circumnavigation — few ultras cover an entire country's border this thoroughly
Real progression path via shorter distances (Challenge 300, 1/4 RAP, 1/2 RAP) below the full 3,600km distance
Genuine WUCA world-championship depth on the 3,600km solo-unsupported category
Both supported and unsupported options across all main distances
weaknesses: Small field (100 athletes total in 2026) — not a mass-participation event despite its scale of distance
Open traffic and variable road quality across 3,600km, with the unsupported category offering only limited intervention if things go wrong
The organizer's own site disagrees with itself on category count (seven per the rules, 'eight solo&teams' on the homepage)
No official 2027 date published as of this review
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, serious ultracycling national-circumnavigation race, best understood and scored as an ultra-format event rather than a conventional gran fondo.

final_verdict.one_liner: Genuine 3,600km/33,100m national circumnavigation ultra with WUCA championship status — a small serious field, not a mass fondo.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're specifically seeking an extreme ultracycling national-circumnavigation challenge and can commit to the solo or team logistics. Not a fit if you're looking for a conventional single-day, mass-participation gran fondo.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a comparable European national-circumnavigation ultra, see Race Around Austria (also in this catalog). For a conventional single-day Polish road gran fondo, look elsewhere in the catalog.
